overview.description: A tabbed interface that allows users to navigate between different views or sections of content within the same page.
overview.purpose: A tabbed interface that allows users to navigate between different views or sections of content within the same page.
whenToUse: When you need to display multiple views or sections of content in a compact space.
whenNotToUse: For single-view interfaces or when a simple dropdown menu suffices.

advanced.SmallViewport:
// JavaScript import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-tab-bar'; import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-tab'; // HTML
Info page with lots of information about us.
Work page that showcases our work.
Hobby page that shows our interests.
Contact page that shows our contacts.
Address page that shows our addresses.

advanced.DisabledTab:
// JavaScript import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-tab-bar'; import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-tab'; // HTML
Info page with lots of information about us.
Prices info
Overdue info
Overview Info
advanced.WithoutPanelSlot:
// JavaScript
import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-tab-bar';
import '@maersk-global/mds-components-core/mc-tab';

// HTML
<mc-tab-bar>
  <!-- tab 0: -->
  <mc-tab slot="tab" label="Info" icon="info-circle"></mc-tab>
  <!-- tab 1: -->
  <mc-tab slot="tab" label="Work" icon="globe"></mc-tab>
  <!-- tab 2: -->
  <mc-tab slot="tab" label="Hobby" icon="heart"></mc-tab>
  <!-- tab 3: -->
  <mc-tab slot="tab" label="Contact" icon="envelope"></mc-tab>
  <!-- tab 4: -->
  <mc-tab slot="tab" label="Address" icon="warehouse"></mc-tab>
</mc-tab-bar>

props.name: currentindex
props.description: The index number of the current `active` item. Index starts from 0

events.name: tabchange
events.description: Emitted when the any of the tabs is selected.

slots.description: Use the tab named slot, when you want to pass individual tab buttons to the tab-bar. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: tab

slots.description: Use the panel named slot, when you want to specify the HTML content that will be shown when a tab button is clicked. Keep in mind that it must be positioned as a node next to the corresponding tab button.. Check stories under Examples folder to see live example.
slots.name: panel
slots.example: The HTML content of the first tab.

members.name: currentindex
members.description:
The index number of the current `active` item.
Index starts from `0`.

cssParts.description: Using this CSS part you can change the look of the buttons wrapper inside the mc-tab-bar component. Check Examples -> Customised appearance story to see live example.
cssParts.name: tabs

cssParts.description: Using this CSS part you can change the look of the panels wrapper inside the mc-tab-bar component. Check Examples -> Customised appearance story to see live example.
cssParts.name: panels
